title: Check Compost Temperature
description: Measure the heat of your compost to ensure active decomposition.

dialogue.text: Wondering if your compost is cooking? Let's find out and react to whatever the dial says.
Insert a compost thermometer deep into the bucket to read the core heat. Wipe the probe afterward so it stays accurate.
What did you see? Hot is great, warm means it's slowing, and cool means it needs more air or browns.
Nice! 55–65 °C means microbes are feasting. Keep the lid cracked to vent steam and plan to turn it soon.

options.text: I'll keep an eye on it.

dialogue.text: A warm reading means fuel is running low. Stir in shredded paper or dry leaves, then check again tomorrow.
Cool temps usually mean compacted material. Break up clumps, add a splash of water, and turn the pile for airflow.
Keep monitoring weekly and turning as needed to maintain that microbial frenzy.
